label: Persepolis
sub: Achaemenid ceremonial capital (518–330 BCE). Construction begun by Darius I; burned by Alexander the Great 330 BCE. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Pasargadae
sub: First Achaemenid capital, built by Cyrus the Great (~546 BCE). Contains the Tomb of Cyrus. UNESCO World Heritage Site.

label: Susa (Shush)
sub: One of the world's oldest cities (4200 BCE). Winter capital of the Achaemenid Empire. UNESCO World Heritage Site 2015.

label: Hamadan (Ecbatana)
sub: Ancient capital of the Medes (~678 BCE). Summer residence of Achaemenid, Parthian and Sassanid kings. Tomb of Esther and Mordechai.

label: Isfahan
sub: Safavid imperial capital (1598–1736). Naqsh-e Jahan Square (UNESCO). Called 'half the world' in Persian proverb; pop. 2.2 million today.

label: Tehran
sub: Capital of Iran since 1796. Seat of the Supreme Leader, President, and Parliament. Golestan Palace (UNESCO). Population ~10 million metro area.

label: Tabriz
sub: Historic capital of Iranian Azerbaijan. Bazaar (UNESCO). Major center of the 1906 Constitutional Revolution. Gateway city to Turkey and the Caucasus.

label: Mashhad
sub: Iran's holiest city. Shrine of Imam Reza (8th Shia Imam). 25+ million pilgrims/year. Capital of Khorasan-e Razavi province.

label: Qom
sub: Iran's center of Shia Islamic scholarship. Hundreds of seminaries (hawzas). Khomeini's home base before exile. Fordow nuclear facility 30km north.

label: Shiraz
sub: City of poets and wine. Tombs of Hafez and Sa'di. Gateway to Persepolis (60km). Cultural capital of the Zand dynasty (18th century).

label: Natanz Enrichment Facility
sub: Main uranium enrichment site. Underground fuel enrichment plant with 19,000 centrifuges. Targeted by Stuxnet 2010, sabotage 2021. IAEA-monitored.

label: Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant
sub: Deeply buried (80m underground) enrichment site near Qom. Revealed to IAEA by Iran 2009. JCPOA converted to research center; Iran resumed 20% enrichment 2021.

label: Arak Heavy Water Reactor
sub: IR-40 heavy water research reactor. Plutonium production risk. JCPOA required redesign to eliminate plutonium production pathway. Redesign ongoing.

label: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant
sub: Iran's only operational nuclear power plant. 1,000 MW. Built with Russian assistance (Rosatom). IAEA-safeguarded. Online since 2011. Civilian use only.

label: Isfahan Nuclear Technology Center / UCF
sub: Uranium Conversion Facility (UCF). Converts yellowcake to UF6 gas feed for centrifuges. IAEA-monitored. Iran resumed conversion here in 2005 after EU-3 suspension.

label: Ahvaz / Khuzestan Province
sub: Heart of Iran's oil industry. Site of Iran-Iraq War battles (1980–88). Khuzestan produces ~80% of Iran's oil. Major Arab-minority region; separatist tensions.

label: Bandar Abbas
sub: Iran's largest port. Strategic location on Strait of Hormuz. Shah Abbas I captured from Portuguese 1622. Major IRGC naval base. Key to oil export and imports.

label: Abadan Oil Refinery
sub: Once world's largest oil refinery (British-built, 1909). Nationalized by Mosaddegh 1951. Heavily damaged during Iran-Iraq War siege (1980–82). Rebuilt after 1988.

label: Khorramshahr ('City of Blood')
sub: Site of Iran-Iraq War's bloodiest urban battle (Sept–Oct 1980). Iraq captures after 34 days. Iran reconquers in Operation Beit ol-Moqaddas (May 24, 1982). 7,000+ killed.

label: Khark Island Oil Terminal
sub: Iran's main crude oil export terminal in the Persian Gulf. Handles ~90% of Iranian oil exports. Repeatedly targeted by Iraq during the 'Tanker War' (1984–88).

label: Masjed Soleyman — First Oil Well
sub: Site of first commercial oil strike in Middle East (May 26, 1908) by British engineer George Reynolds. Launch point of the modern Iranian oil industry and APOC (later BP).

label: Yazd — Zoroastrian Center
sub: Iran's best-preserved ancient city. Home of the largest Zoroastrian community in Iran. Eternal flame at Atashkadeh has burned for 1,500 years. UNESCO World Heritage 2017.

label: Chabahar Port
sub: Iran's only deep-water port on the Indian Ocean (Gulf of Oman). India-Iran joint development. US sanctions exemption granted 2018–2023. Strategic access to Afghanistan bypassing Pakistan.

label: Sanandaj (Kurdistan Province)
sub: Capital of Kurdistan Province. Site of PJAK (Iranian Kurdish) insurgency. Mahsa Amini's hometown of Saqqez is nearby. Periodic IRGC military operations.

label: Mahabad — Republic of Mahabad (1946)
sub: Site of the short-lived Soviet-backed Republic of Mahabad (Jan–Dec 1946) — first Kurdish autonomous state. Crushed by Iranian forces after Soviet withdrawal. Kurdish national symbol.

label: Zahedan (Sistan-Baluchestan)
sub: Capital of Iran's most restive province. Jaish al-Adl (Sunni Baluchi militant group) operations. Sept 2022 Bloody Friday: IRGC kills 90+ protesters in mosque shooting.

label: Naqsh-e Rostam — Royal Achaemenid Tombs
sub: Cliff tombs of Darius I, Xerxes I, Artaxerxes I, and Darius II. Sassanid rock reliefs including Shapur I's capture of Roman Emperor Valerian (260 CE). Near Persepolis.

label: Evin Prison, Tehran
sub: Iran's most notorious political prison. Houses journalists, activists, dual nationals, and political opponents. Known for torture; Amnesty International and HRW document abuses.

label: Naqsh-e Jahan Square, Isfahan
sub: World's second-largest public square (512m × 163m). Built by Shah Abbas I (1598–1629). Imam Mosque, Sheikh Lotfollah Mosque, Ali Qapu Palace. UNESCO World Heritage 1979.

label: Faw Peninsula — Iran-Iraq War
sub: Iran captures Faw Peninsula in Operation Dawn 8 (Feb 1986), cutting Iraq from Persian Gulf. Iraq retakes in Operation Ramadan el-Mubarak (April 1988) with chemical weapons and Western intelligence.

label: Behistun (Bisotun) Inscription
sub: Monumental trilingual inscription of Darius I (c. 520 BCE) in Old Persian, Elamite, and Babylonian — key to deciphering cuneiform. UNESCO World Heritage 2006.

label: Chogha Zanbil Ziggurat
sub: Largest surviving ziggurat outside Mesopotamia (c. 1250 BCE). Built by Elamite king Untash-Napirisha as a sacred city. UNESCO World Heritage 1979 — Iran's first UNESCO listing. Located in Khuzestan province.

label: Takht-e Soleyman (Throne of Solomon)
sub: Sassanid royal and Zoroastrian fire temple complex (3rd–7th century CE). Built around a volcanic lake; the sacred fire here was carried to Persepolis. UNESCO World Heritage 2003. West Azerbaijan province.

label: Sardasht — First Civilian Chemical Attack
sub: June 28, 1987: Iraqi aircraft dropped mustard gas bombs on this Kurdish city in West Azerbaijan — the first confirmed use of chemical weapons directly targeting a civilian population. 130 killed, ~8,000 injured; many survivors suffered lifelong health effects.

label: Golestan Palace, Tehran
sub: Royal Qajar palace complex (18th–20th century). UNESCO World Heritage 2013. Site of Reza Khan's coronation as Reza Shah (1925) and Mohammad Reza Shah's coronation (1967). Emblem of the Pahlavi monarchy now managed as a museum.

label: Kermanshah — Taq-e Bostan Rock Reliefs
sub: Sassanid monumental rock carvings (4th–7th century CE) depicting investiture of Ardashir II and Khosrow II. Gateway city to the Zagros Mountains and western Iran. Population 1 million; major oil refinery hub.

label: Lake Urmia — Environmental Crisis
sub: Once the Middle East's largest saltwater lake (5,200 km²). Has shrunk ~90% since the 1970s due to damming, irrigation diversion, and climate change. UN declared it an environmental emergency. Toxic salt dust from exposed lakebed affects 14 million people.

label: Parchin Military Complex
sub: Large military–industrial compound 30km southeast of Tehran. IAEA inspectors sought access from 2003, suspecting high-explosive tests relevant to nuclear weapons development. Iran denied access; satellite imagery showed site modifications. Considered a key site of PMD (Possible Military Dimensions) investigation.

label: Khomein — Khomeini's Birthplace
sub: Small city in central Iran (Markazi province) where Ruhollah Khomeini was born September 24, 1902. Now a pilgrimage site with museum. Renamed Khomeini's birthplace after the revolution; contains the house where the future Ayatollah grew up.

label: Dezful — 'City of Blood' (Iran-Iraq War)
sub: Khuzestan city that sustained the most prolonged missile bombardment of the Iran-Iraq War (1980–88) — over 300 Scud and Al-Hussein missile strikes. Site of Operation Karbala-5 (1987). Its resistance became a symbol of Iranian steadfastness.

label: Rasht — Caspian Gateway
sub: Capital of Gilan province on the Caspian Sea coast. Iran's rainiest city. Site of the Soviet-backed Persian Soviet Republic (June 1920). Historically cosmopolitan trade city linked to Russia. Key node on emerging North–South Transport Corridor (Russia–India via Iran).
